summary.narrative: Mayor Fulop has a mixed record on campaign promises. While he achieved several years without direct tax increases and made progress on development initiatives, his stormwater fee was criticized as a 'backdoor tax' and was ultimately suspended. His focus has increasingly shifted toward his 2025 gubernatorial campaign.

promises.text: No tax increases for Jersey City residents
promises.evidence: While Fulop claims eight years of no tax increases, the 2020 stormwater utility fee was widely criticized as a 'backdoor tax' that overcharged residents, contradicting his promise of no tax increases that year.

promises.text: Keep Jersey City affordable for residents
promises.evidence: Fulop has stated affordability was a 'main priority' and avoided direct property tax increases, but rising costs and the stormwater fee controversy undermine full delivery on this promise.

promises.text: Implement stormwater utility fee with nominal charges
promises.evidence: The stormwater fee approved in 2020 resulted in residents 'being way overcharged on what was supposed to be a nominal fee' according to Fulop himself, leading to its suspension before the 2021 elections.

promises.text: Reevaluate and fix the stormwater fee formula
promises.evidence: After suspending the fee in 2021, Fulop promised the formula would be reevaluated. Progress on implementing a revised fee structure remains unclear.

promises.text: Maintain fiscal stability for Jersey City
promises.evidence: Fulop stated the city was left 'very stable' financially, and Jersey City has maintained its credit ratings and avoided major fiscal crises during his tenure.
Fulop launched his 2025 gubernatorial bid in April 2023, promising a campaign 'more throughout around public policy' and has engaged substantively on policy issues at campaign events.

promises.text: Prioritize public safety and reduce crime in Jersey City
promises.evidence: Jersey City has seen reductions in violent crime during Fulop's tenure, with investments in police technology and community policing initiatives.
